identity.canonical_title: Divine Ideograms The Sacred Hand Gestures of

theory.canonical_statement: The fist gesture is treated as a source-backed ideogram mapping to the Sumerian bond/seal readings DUR and KISIB.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry is handled as a finite mapping from a manual gesture class to a set of source-backed readings. The Wolfram model stores the same relation as an association.

reviewed_source_symbols.description: manual gesture class named by the source evidence.
finite lexical mapping from a gesture class to source-backed transliteration readings.
set of normalized transliteration readings attached to the gesture.

dictionary_sections.definition: Divine Ideograms The Sacred Hand Gestures of is a source-backed lexical mapping entry. It converts a manual gesture into a controlled symbolic relation rather than treating the source HTML heading as an equation.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In the production registry, the gesture Fist is represented by the transliteration set DUR, KISIB. This lets the Sumerian material live in the same four-page layout as mathematical entries while preserving the fact that the object is a semiotic mapping.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The mapping separates gesture, transliteration, and meaning: DUR: bond; KISIB: seal. It gives the entry a stable symbolic spine for indexing, visual glyph work, and later source review.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: Gesture-to-sign mapping; transliteration normalization; lexical source grounding; symbolic registry encoding.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: The entry is handled as a finite mapping from a manual gesture class to a set of source-backed readings. The Wolfram model stores the same relation as an association.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: Source grounding: `D:/00_Engine/CSV/TZPID_latex_compile_list_oovol00_priority_master_usable.csv`. Source excerpt: The Fist: DUR (The Bond) or KISIB (The Seal). The production canonical relation is \mathrm{Fist}\mapsto\{\mathrm{DUR},\mathrm{KISIB}\}.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This entry anchors the Sumerian gesture lexicon inside the registry without allowing malformed HTML headings to enter the canonical equation field.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Gesture Mapping: The manual form `Fist` represented as a registry relation.
\item Transliteration Set: The readings `DUR, KISIB` carried by the source evidence.
\item Lexical Symbol Key: The separation of gesture, reading, and meaning for later glyph-quality work.
\end{enumerate}
dictionary_sections.encyclopedia_mathematica: Divine Ideograms The Sacred Hand Gestures of should be indexed as a semiotic mapping rather than a physical equation. Its diagram should show the gesture node, the transliteration nodes, and the meaning labels as a small graph.

publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: The mapping separates gesture, transliteration, and meaning: DUR: bond; KISIB: seal. It gives the entry a stable symbolic spine for indexing, visual glyph work, and later source review.
